notes: FROZEN — agent_supports: yes / daemon_capability: incapable / driver_capability: ready → route frozen. kiro-cli 2.5.1 CAN pin a non-default model (launch flag `kiro-cli chat --model <id>`, the in-REPL /model picker, `--list-models` — FINDINGS.md §8), so the agent performs model identification. But the model never reaches a Source the daemon tails: the watched *.jsonl event vocab (Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear, FINDINGS.md §4) carries NO model field, and kirocli/parser.go sets ev.ModelName on no path (grep ModelName over the adapter is empty; parser.go:20-22 — the model lives only in the <uuid>.json sidecar this parser does not read). So SessionMetrics.ModelName stays 'unknown' and ContextWindow cannot resolve from the transcript — the scenario's two observables (ModelName == launch model, not 'unknown'; ContextWindow == the published window) are unrecoverable from the watched Source → daemon incapable, NOT bug (no trace is mis-handled; the model trace is simply absent from the tailed JSONL). Contrast: aider records the model IN the transcript (`> Model:` blockquote, modelRE) and pi via message.model + model_change.modelId → both daemon: full; kiro-cli has no in-transcript analogue. No state phases are asserted: with no model signal in the transcript there is nothing for the verify engine to check against the recording. The observation vector is documented for completeness — model is unknown (no model field in the JSONL), cost/tokens are zero (no token/cost field in the JSONL; sidecar token counts are present-but-0). Unblock (documented, not tonight): a kirocli sidecar MetricsReader over <uuid>.json session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.{model_id,context_window_tokens} (confirmed in the live smoke sidecar: model_id='auto', context_window_tokens=1000000) → SessionMetrics.{ModelName,ContextWindow}, flipping daemon incapable→full and re-routing this cell to record; a recording would then need an explicit --model launch so model_id pins a concrete (non-'auto') id.
